title: Gerund after remember
body: Here, remember is followed by **-ing** because you recall a past experience (you remember doing the activity). With **to + verb**, remember means remembering to do something in the future.

title: Enjoy + -ing
body: **Enjoy** is typically followed by a gerund (**-ing**), not by an infinitive.

title: Decide + to infinitive
body: **Decide** is followed by **to + base verb** to express a decision about a future action.

title: Stop + to infinitive (purpose)
body: **Stopped to eat** means we stopped for the purpose of eating. **Stop + -ing** means quitting an ongoing activity. The infinitive shows **purpose** after **stop**.

title: Forget + to infinitive
body: **Don't forget to practice** refers to something to do later. **Forget + -ing** means forgetting something that happened (a memory).
hint: Future task not done → forget + to + verb.

title: Verb pattern type
body: **Stop** can take both **-ing** and **to** infinitive; the meaning changes with the structure.
hint: This verb is in the “both / meaning changes” group.

title: Correct
body: You picked the right form. Read the explanation below to lock in the rule.

title: Not quite
body: Compare your answer with the correct pattern and review the feedback below.
